real_comment:
ふみ先生、メリークリスマス🎅🏼🥧🍰
フライドチキンは美味しそうですね。 これはやっぱりおいしいビールが合うと思います🍻😁

時間が過ぎるのは早いですよね! あっという間の1年でした。あと1週間2025年になります。先生のチャンネルを見つけてから三年半が経ちました。この間、動画を通して美しい景色を見たり、日本の文化と生活について学んだりすることができ、とても役に立ちました。そして、素敵なお声を聞きながら、シャドーイングの練習をしていて、それは自分の発音を改善するために確かに良い方法ですね🤗 ふみ先生のおかげで、日本語を勉強するだけでなく、本当に楽しんでいますね！いつもすばらしい動画を作っていただき、大変ありがとうございました🙇🏻

さて、「機会は努力する者を訪れ、怠ける者からは去っていく。」この言葉は私の2024年のカレンダーの月間標語から学びました。本当にその通りだと思っています。先月、先生のチャンネル登録者数は30万人を超えて、おめでとうございます✨🎊この数字を見て本当にうれしかったです。めっちゃすごいね👍👏先生のチャンネルは日本語を勉強している私たちにとって欠かせない存在です。私の場合は間違いなくこれを勉強し続けます。次の3つのことわざはいつもわたしの心の中で覚えて、やっていることです。

1) 「塵も積もれば山となる」一日一日少しずつ勉強しています。

2) 「継続は力なり」ずっと続けたいと思っている学習です。

3) 「習うより慣れろ」毎日、日本に関するニュースなどを読むとか、テレビを見るとかが続いています。そして、同僚はみんな日本語を勉強しているので、日本語の会話を練習する機会でもあります。

来年もよろしくお願い致します🙏🏻
お互いに頑張りましょう💪🏻

健康で幸せでありますように❣️
良いお年をお迎えください🌈

real_comment: Supermarkets in New Zealand start with fruit and vegetables too. I like the summer fruits more than winter ones so I don't eat a lot of fruit in winter. Christmas here is different. First off it's summer because Southern Hemisphere so it's really hot. A lot of people go to the beach or have a barbeque. My family are all Christians and go to church in the morning, open presents (either before or after church) and we have Christmas food at lunch then leftover food for dinner. One of my friends families has KFC but get it the day before as most places are closed on Christmas. It is celebrated on the 25th December. We also go look at lights. They usually have a map of houses that are decorated in the local newspaper. You have to wait until 9pm at least to look as it's still too light outside. I'm not a Christian so I don't go with my family to church and I don't like Christmas. It's too stressful and the songs are bad. I have had a relaxing Christmas away from my family and it was good. I still don't like it but it was way better being around my friends
translated_comment: Supermarkets in New Zealand start with fruit and vegetables too. I like the summer fruits more than winter ones so I don't eat a lot of fruit in winter. Christmas here is different. First off it's summer because Southern Hemisphere so it's really hot. A lot of people go to the beach or have a barbeque. My family are all Christians and go to church in the morning, open presents (either before or after church) and we have Christmas food at lunch then leftover food for dinner. One of my friends families has KFC but get it the day before as most places are closed on Christmas. It is celebrated on the 25th December. We also go look at lights. They usually have a map of houses that are decorated in the local newspaper. You have to wait until 9pm at least to look as it's still too light outside. I'm not a Christian so I don't go with my family to church and I don't like Christmas. It's too stressful and the songs are bad. I have had a relaxing Christmas away from my family and it was good. I still don't like it but it was way better being around my friends

real_comment: This video is very interesting and relaxing. In austria we celebrate christmas at the 24th december. We have the tradition of baking a lot of christmas cookies even a month before that day, they are very yummy, my favorite is Vanillekipferl. In the morning we make the christmas tree all pretty, we put on the lights and Christbaumkugeln. We listen to christmas songs or watch some tv and relax. In the afternoon we cook fried or baked carp, most of the time it has a breading (Panade). We eat it with potato salad. I think carp is preffered for christmas here, but sometimes people also eat goose. Some families go to church in the evening, we pray and sing. Most of the time there are children in the church who will show a theatre play of maria and josef in bethlehem. Then we go home and exchange presents.
translated_comment: This video is very interesting and relaxing. In austria we celebrate christmas at the 24th december. We have the tradition of baking a lot of christmas cookies even a month before that day, they are very yummy, my favorite is Vanillekipferl. In the morning we make the christmas tree all pretty, we put on the lights and Christbaumkugeln. We listen to christmas songs or watch some tv and relax. In the afternoon we cook fried or baked carp, most of the time it has a breading (Panade). We eat it with potato salad. I think carp is preffered for christmas here, but sometimes people also eat goose. Some families go to church in the evening, we pray and sing. Most of the time there are children in the church who will show a theatre play of maria and josef in bethlehem. Then we go home and exchange presents.

translated_comment: Thank you for this great listening video!  Your Japanese is so clear and easy to understand!  I just found and subscribed to your channel, so I plan to listen to it a few more times as well as your other videos.   I've lived in Kanagawa for 4.5 years with my Japanese wife.  She wants to speak better English and I want to speak better Japanese, so we try to balance our language use.   Thank you again!  To answer  your question, my wife and I are Christian, so we typically celebrate by decorating our house (inside and out) with many Christmas decorations and a Christmas tree.  We typically exchange a few gifts on Christmas morning, but we didn't do that this year since we bought ourselves a new car.  haha   I love to roast a turkey for dinner, but since moving to Japan, we don't have a large oven, so now we have a smaller meal or go to a restaurant.  Happy New Year!

real_comment: こんにちは ふみさん. Watching from America. I have always been interested in Japanese culture, music and entertainment since I was 5. Now I am 27 and I am learning Japanese. I can speak to some level but I have struggled with learning a new language since I speak 3 different languages. I was also afraid of learning Japanese especially with how difficult it is to read kanji. I have learned hiragana and katakana in the past. But I just started learning kanji recently to fulfill my dream to help me read without a translator. Grateful to find a youtuber that provides practice with conversations but also the kanji and hiragana equivalent for the phrases. Thank you for taking the time to upload your videos. You are one of the best and hopefully with your videos, I can return to Japan and be able to have conversations.
translated_comment: こんにちは ふみさん. Watching from America. I have always been interested in Japanese culture, music and entertainment since I was 5. Now I am 27 and I am learning Japanese. I can speak to some level but I have struggled with learning a new language since I speak 3 different languages. I was also afraid of learning Japanese especially with how difficult it is to read kanji. I have learned hiragana and katakana in the past. But I just started learning kanji recently to fulfill my dream to help me read without a translator. Grateful to find a youtuber that provides practice with conversations but also the kanji and hiragana equivalent for the phrases. Thank you for taking the time to upload your videos. You are one of the best and hopefully with your videos, I can return to Japan and be able to have conversations.

real_comment: 友達が同じブランドの納豆を買うように言いました。 今日は朝食に納豆とご飯を食べます！お疲れ様でした！私はあなたの買い物のルーチンを見るのが好きです。In the US, I live in a smaller city, our shopping carts usually get too full and you have to use your car to fill up all your groceries. But now that I am living in Japan, I usually buy groceries every few days based on what I want to cook because I have to carry everything in a tote bag and walk home. I have to use a translation app whenever I buy packaged products and I don’t know what is inside. It’s a fun experience. But I enjoy shopping and use it as a opportunity to learn the Japanese language.
